title: Tool
description: Top-level metadata for a tool in the containers-db catalog. Mirrors the contents of `tools/<id>/index.json`. One file per tool. Per-version data lives in sibling `versions/<v>.json` files validated by version.schema.json. The `kind: system_package` variant is a tracking record only — it carries a `system_package` sub-object instead of `default_version`/`available` (no `versions/` subdirectory) and is referenced from version files via `Dependency.tool`.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version for the tool index document. Bumped on breaking shape changes; consumers reject unknown versions.

id.description: Stable, lowercase, snake-case slug. Must match the parent directory name (`tools/<id>/`). Referenced by other tool entries' ordering arrays and by version files' `tool` field.

display_name.description: Human-readable name shown in stibbons UIs and recommendation output. Free-form, mixed case allowed.

kind.description: Coarse category. `language` covers programming-language toolchains (Rust, Python, Node). `cli` is end-user binaries (`gh`, `just`). `library` is build-time/runtime libs without an entry-point binary. `runtime` covers things like JVM/V8 that host other code. `service` is long-running daemons (Postgres, Redis). `system_package` is a tracking record for an OS package the host package manager owns (apt/apk/yum); the catalog records its existence and cross-distro name map but never `picks` a version.

homepage.description: Canonical project homepage URL. Used by stibbons in `info` output.

source_repo.description: Primary source repository URL (the repo the activity scanner watches).

license.description: SPDX identifier of the project's license (e.g., `MIT`, `Apache-2.0`, `GPL-3.0-or-later`). Free-form so dual-licensed and exotic licenses can be expressed.

activity.description: Activity scoring snapshot from the most recent scan. The `score` tier drives stibbons' recommendation gating; the `signals` block records the raw inputs so future re-evaluation against new heuristics is possible without re-fetching.

score.description: Coarse activity bucket. The 7 tiers (most → least active): `very-active`, `active`, `maintained`, `slow`, `stale`, `dormant`, `abandoned`. stibbons recommends only `maintained` and above; `slow`/`stale` warn; `dormant`/`abandoned` refuse.

signals.description: Raw scanner observations that produced the score. Optional fields — scanners record what they can fetch from the source forge.

releases_last_90d.description: Count of tagged/published releases in the trailing 90-day window.

commits_last_90d.description: Count of commits to the default branch in the trailing 90-day window.

active_maintainers.description: Count of distinct authors who committed in the trailing 90-day window. Proxy for bus factor.

open_advisories.description: Count of open security advisories on the source forge (e.g., GHSA entries) at scan time.

last_release_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent release (any channel).

last_commit_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent commit on the default branch.

scan_cadence_days.description: Days between scans for this tool. Set by the scanner based on `score` (very-active=1, active=7, maintained=30, slow/stale=90 — guideline only). Not enum-locked: the recommendation engine (#404) may refine.

scanned_at.description: When the scanner last produced this snapshot. Consumers can compare against `scan_cadence_days` to detect stale data.

validation_tiers.description: Per-tool documentation of which verification tiers (1-4) the install methods support, mirroring the 4-tier model in `lib/base/checksum-verification.sh`. Tier 1 = signatures (GPG/sigstore), Tier 2 = pinned checksums, Tier 3 = published checksums, Tier 4 = TOFU. The actual per-method encoding lives on `install_methods[].verification` in version files; this object is the catalog-level summary so a recommender can answer 'does this tool support Tier 2 verification?' without scanning every version.

version_style.description: How this tool's version strings should be parsed. `semver` is the implicit default when omitted; `prefix` is for tools that use a leading `v` or similar token; `calver` is for date-derived schemes; `opaque` disables comparator parsing entirely (constraints involving this tool can only check string equality).

default_version.description: The version stibbons selects when no version is requested. Free-form string so partial versions (e.g., Python `3.12`) are allowed alongside exact pins. Required for every kind except `system_package` (the host package manager picks).

minimum_recommended.description: Lowest version stibbons will offer when recommending. Versions older than this are still installable on explicit request, but excluded from defaults and warnings flagged.

channels.description: Named release channels the tool publishes (e.g., `stable`, `beta`, `nightly`). Keys are channel ids referenced from version files' `channel` field. Cross-reference enforcement is the consumer's job — JSON Schema only checks shape.

description.description: Human-readable summary of what this channel contains and its support guarantees.

default.description: When true, this is the channel `default_version` resolves against. Exactly one channel should be marked default; not enforced by schema.

ordering.description: Install-order constraints for this tool relative to other catalog tools. luggage's resolver topologically sorts using these arrays.

must_install_before.description: Hard constraint: every listed tool id must be installed before this one. Cycles are rejected by the resolver.

should_install_before.description: Soft constraint: prefer to install the listed tool ids before this one, but the resolver may reorder to break cycles or honor scheduling.

alternatives.description: Advisory pointers to related tools (similar capabilities, partial overlap, succession). Never auto-substituted by the resolver — surfaces in remediation menus and `stibbons info` output only. Direction is from this tool TO the listed alternative; `successor` means `the listed tool came after this one`.

relationship.description: Nature of the relationship. `similar` = same problem solved with comparable tradeoffs; `overlaps` = partial functional overlap; `successor` = supersedes this tool; `predecessor` = this tool supersedes the listed one.

scope.description: Optional narrowing of where the relationship applies (e.g., `linux-only`, `cli-only`). Free-form.

notes.description: Free-form reviewer notes for the remediation menu.

system_package.description: Cross-distro identification for a `kind: system_package` tracking record. Maps each supported distro to the package name that distro's package manager uses. luggage looks this up at install time when a Dependency targets this entry. Only meaningful when `kind == system_package` — the schema's `allOf` block forbids the field on other kinds and requires it here. Trust source is intentionally absent: apt/apk/yum's signed-repo configuration on the host is the trust root, not the catalog.

platforms.description: Map of distro id → per-distro package name. Distros not present in the map are unsupported for this system_package; consumer Dependency entries that narrow `platforms` to an unmapped distro are rejected by the cross-ref validator.

name.description: Package name as it appears to the distro's package manager (e.g., `libc6-dev` on Debian, `glibc-devel` on RHEL).

available.description: Catalog of versions known to this tool entry. The list is the source of truth for `luggage list-versions`. Each entry may carry `last_known_good_for` fossil records describing the most recent OS/distro version on which this tool version was tested before support was dropped. Required for every kind except `system_package` (host package manager owns version selection, no per-version files exist).

version.description: Version string. Must match the basename of a sibling `versions/<v>.json` file when one exists.

last_known_good_for.description: Fossil map of distro id → highest distro version where this tool version was last tested working. Captures the moment a newer tool version dropped a distro so consumers can find the last working pair.

then.description: system_package: tracking record only. Requires `system_package`; forbids `default_version` and `available` (no version selection, no versions/ subdirectory).

else.description: Standard tools (language/cli/library/runtime/service): require `default_version` + `available`; forbid the `system_package` sub-object (reserved for tracking records).

TierSummary.description: Catalog-level note about whether a verification tier is available for this tool. Per-method specifics live on version files.

available.description: True when at least one install method on at least one supported version uses this tier.

notes.description: Free-form reviewer notes (e.g., upstream signing key URL, reasons a tier is unavailable).
